# Polychorus Actor Development Guide

> Build a visual actor for the Polychorus collaborative art installation. Actors are TypeScript modules that draw on a shared 2D canvas using a sandboxed API. Deploy your actor live at CloudFest Hackathon 2026.

## What is an Actor?

An actor is a self-contained visual module that draws on a shared canvas. Multiple actors paint simultaneously — your actor will collaborate with others to create unique artworks. The runtime renders at 60fps using Pixi.js 8.x at 360x640 pixels (9:16 portrait).

## Requirements

- Node.js >= 22.0.0
- pnpm >= 9.0.0

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone <repository-url>
cd art-installation
pnpm install
pnpm new:actor my-actor-name
# Edit actors/community/my-actor-name/src/index.ts
pnpm dev  # Visit http://localhost:3000?actor=my-actor-name
```

## Actor Interface

```typescript
import { registerActor } from '@art/actor-sdk';
import type { Actor, ActorMetadata, ActorSetupAPI, ActorUpdateAPI, FrameContext } from '@art/types';

const metadata: ActorMetadata = {
  id: 'my-actor-name',           // Unique kebab-case ID
  name: 'My Actor Name',         // Display name
  description: 'What it does',   // Short description
  author: { name: 'Your Name', github: 'your-github' },
  version: '1.0.0',              // Semver
  tags: ['generative', 'particles'],
  createdAt: new Date(),
  preferredDuration: 30,          // Seconds
  requiredContexts: ['time'],     // Optional: 'time' | 'weather' | 'audio' | 'video' | 'social'
};

const actor: Actor = {
  metadata,

  // Optional: called once when actor loads
  async setup(api: ActorSetupAPI): Promise<void> {
    const { width, height } = api.canvas.getSize();
    // Initialize state, pre-allocate arrays
  },

  // REQUIRED: called every frame (~60fps)
  update(api: ActorUpdateAPI, frame: FrameContext): void {
    // frame.time = elapsed ms, frame.deltaTime = ms since last frame
    // Draw using api.brush.*, read canvas with api.canvas.*
  },

  // Optional: cleanup when deactivated
  async teardown(): Promise<void> {},
};

registerActor(actor);
export default actor;
```

## Actor Roles

| Role | Description | Count per Cycle |
|------|-------------|-----------------|
| `foreground` | Standard drawing actors (default) | 2-5 per cycle |
| `background` | Renders behind everything | Exactly 1 |
| `filter` | Post-processing effects | 0-2 per layer |

Set role in metadata: `role: 'background'` or `role: 'filter'`. Default is `foreground`.

## Drawing API (api.brush)

### Shapes
```typescript
api.brush.circle(x, y, radius, style?)
api.brush.ellipse(x, y, width, height, style?)
api.brush.rect(x, y, width, height, style?)
api.brush.polygon(points, style?)          // points: {x, y}[]
api.brush.star(x, y, outerR, innerR, numPoints, style?)
```

### Lines
```typescript
api.brush.line(x1, y1, x2, y2, style?)
api.brush.stroke(points, style?)           // points: {x, y}[]
api.brush.bezier(start, cp1, cp2, end, style?)
```

### Text and Images
```typescript
api.brush.text('Hello', x, y, style?)
api.brush.image(src, x, y, options?)       // src: URL or data URL
```

### Transforms
```typescript
api.brush.pushMatrix()    // Save transform state
api.brush.popMatrix()     // Restore transform state
api.brush.translate(x, y)
api.brush.rotate(angle)   // Radians
api.brush.scale(sx, sy?)
```

### ShapeStyle
```typescript
{
  fill: '#ff6600',         // Color string, number (0xff6600), or gradient
  stroke: '#ffffff',       // Stroke color
  strokeWidth: 2,          // Stroke width in pixels
  alpha: 0.7,              // Opacity 0-1
  blendMode: 'add',        // 'normal' | 'add' | 'multiply' | 'screen' | etc.
}
```

### LineStyle
```typescript
{
  color: '#ffffff',        // Color string or number
  width: 2,               // Line width
  alpha: 0.7,             // Opacity
  cap: 'round',           // 'butt' | 'round' | 'square'
}
```

### Gradients (CRITICAL: use 0-1 coordinates)

Gradient coordinates are relative to the shape's bounding box, NOT pixel values.

```typescript
// Radial gradient
api.brush.circle(x, y, 100, {
  fill: {
    type: 'radial',
    cx: 0.5, cy: 0.5, radius: 0.5,  // 0-1 range, centered
    stops: [
      { offset: 0, color: 'rgba(255,255,255,1)' },
      { offset: 1, color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0)' },
    ],
  },
  blendMode: 'add',
});

// Linear gradient
api.brush.rect(x, y, w, h, {
  fill: {
    type: 'linear',
    x0: 0, y0: 0, x1: 1, y1: 1,  // Diagonal
    stops: [
      { offset: 0, color: '#ff0000' },
      { offset: 1, color: '#0000ff' },
    ],
  },
});
```

## Canvas API (api.canvas — read-only)

```typescript
api.canvas.getSize()                     // { width: number, height: number }
api.canvas.getPixel(x, y)               // { r, g, b, a }
api.canvas.getDominantColors(count)      // Color[]
api.canvas.isEmpty(x, y)                // boolean
api.canvas.findEmptyRegions(minSize)     // Rectangle[]
```

## Filter API (api.filter — for filter-role actors)

```typescript
api.filter.blur(amount)
api.filter.brightness(amount)       // 0-2, 1 = normal
api.filter.contrast(amount)
api.filter.saturate(amount)
api.filter.hueRotate(degrees)
api.filter.grayscale(amount?)
api.filter.noise(amount)
api.filter.pixelate(size)
api.filter.glow(color, intensity)
api.filter.vignette(amount)
```

## Context API (api.context — environment data)

```typescript
// Time
api.context.time.hour()              // 0-23
api.context.time.dayProgress()       // 0-1
api.context.time.season()           // 'spring' | 'summer' | 'autumn' | 'winter'

// Weather
api.context.weather.condition()      // 'clear' | 'clouds' | 'rain' | 'snow' | etc.
api.context.weather.temperature()    // Celsius

// Audio
api.context.audio.bass()             // 0-1
api.context.audio.isBeat()           // boolean
api.context.audio.bpm()             // number | null

// Social
api.context.social.sentiment()       // -1 to 1
api.context.social.viewerCount()     // number
```

## Best Practices

### Performance (CRITICAL)
- Keep frame time under 16.67ms for 60fps
- Target <300 draw calls per frame
- Use numeric colors (0xff6600) + separate alpha instead of rgba strings
- Pre-render textures in setup() for repeated elements
- Avoid O(n^2) — use squared distance checks with early-out

### Blend Modes
- NEVER use `api.brush.setBlendMode()` in the update loop — causes flickering
- ALWAYS pass `blendMode` in each shape's style object
- Use 'add' or 'screen' for glow effects on dark backgrounds
- Avoid 'overlay' on dark backgrounds

### Visibility
- Use alpha >= 0.6 for main shapes (lower values are nearly invisible)
- Use stroke widths >= 2.5px for visible lines
- Test on dark backgrounds (canvas is typically dark)
- Skip rendering when alpha < 0.05 (prevents fade-out flickering)

### Transforms
- Always pair pushMatrix() with popMatrix()
- Don't set global state inside a push/pop block

### Memory Management (MANDATORY)
- Pre-allocate arrays/objects in setup(), never in update()
- Use object pools with `active` flag instead of create/destroy
- Cap all collections with MAX constants
- Use circular buffers for history/trail data, not push/shift

```typescript
// BAD — allocates every frame
state.particles.push(createParticle());
state.particles = state.particles.filter(p => p.alive);

// GOOD — uses pre-allocated pool
const p = pool.find(p => !p.active);
if (p) { p.active = true; initParticle(p); }
```

### Animation Patterns
```typescript
// Fade timing: 20% in, 60% full, 20% out
const progress = elapsed / duration;
let alpha = 1;
if (progress < 0.2) alpha = progress / 0.2;
else if (progress > 0.8) alpha = (1 - progress) / 0.2;
```

## Security Constraints

Actors run in a sandbox and CANNOT access:
- Network: fetch, XMLHttpRequest, WebSocket, EventSource
- Storage: localStorage, sessionStorage, indexedDB
- DOM: document (except createElement('canvas')), window (except crypto)
- Dynamic code: eval, new Function, dynamic import()
- Timing: setInterval is forbidden, setTimeout only in teardown

## Submitting Your Actor

1. Create actor: `pnpm new:actor my-actor-name`
2. Implement `actors/community/my-actor-name/src/index.ts`
3. Preview: visit `http://localhost:3000?actor=my-actor-name` while `pnpm dev` is running
4. Validate: `cd actors/community/my-actor-name && pnpm validate`
5. Create a PR adding only your `actors/community/my-actor-name/` folder
6. CI validates and auto-merges — your actor appears on the live display within 30 seconds

## Example: Audio-Reactive Circles

```typescript
import { registerActor } from '@art/actor-sdk';
import type { Actor, ActorUpdateAPI, FrameContext } from '@art/types';

const actor: Actor = {
  metadata: {
    id: 'pulse-circles',
    name: 'Pulse Circles',
    description: 'Circles that pulse with audio',
    author: { name: 'Hacker', github: 'hacker' },
    version: '1.0.0',
    tags: ['audio', 'circles'],
    createdAt: new Date(),
    requiredContexts: ['audio'],
  },
  update(api: ActorUpdateAPI, frame: FrameContext): void {
    const { width, height } = api.canvas.getSize();
    const bass = api.context.audio.bass();
    const time = frame.time / 1000;

    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
      const angle = (i / 5) * Math.PI * 2 + time * 0.5;
      const radius = 80 + bass * 60;
      const x = width / 2 + Math.cos(angle) * radius;
      const y = height / 2 + Math.sin(angle) * radius;
      const size = 15 + bass * 25;
      const hue = (i * 72 + time * 30) % 360;

      api.brush.circle(x, y, size, {
        fill: `hsla(${hue}, 80%, 60%, 0.8)`,
        blendMode: 'add',
      });
    }
  },
};

registerActor(actor);
export default actor;
```

## Example: Weather-Responsive Background

```typescript
import { registerActor } from '@art/actor-sdk';
import type { Actor, ActorUpdateAPI, FrameContext } from '@art/types';

const actor: Actor = {
  metadata: {
    id: 'weather-bg',
    name: 'Weather Background',
    description: 'Background that changes with weather',
    author: { name: 'Hacker', github: 'hacker' },
    version: '1.0.0',
    tags: ['weather', 'background'],
    role: 'background',
    createdAt: new Date(),
    requiredContexts: ['weather', 'time'],
  },
  update(api: ActorUpdateAPI, frame: FrameContext): void {
    const { width, height } = api.canvas.getSize();
    const weather = api.context.weather.condition();
    const dayProgress = api.context.time.dayProgress();

    const colors: Record<string, number> = {
      clear: 0x1a0a2e,
      clouds: 0x1a1a2e,
      rain: 0x0a1a2e,
      snow: 0x1a1a3e,
    };
    const bg = colors[weather] ?? 0x0a0a1e;
    const brightness = 0.3 + dayProgress * 0.4;

    api.brush.rect(0, 0, width, height, {
      fill: bg,
      alpha: brightness,
    });
  },
};

registerActor(actor);
export default actor;
```
